The BlitzMetrics Task Library documents 240 operational tasks across 13 categories — each one a runnable SOP tied to a definitive article. 117 are ready, 100 in progress, 23 identified gaps. Updated July 2026.

Content Factory — Produce

Recording and capturing raw content: videos, photos, stories, presentations

  • Batch record 50 raw clips in one session — Run a volume-first recording session that produces 50 short raw clips in one sitting, filling the Content Factory's intake queue for weeks.
  • Capture client stories on phone — Film real client testimonials and success stories on a smartphone so the brand has authentic third-party proof to process, post, and promote.
  • Create a 3 minute why video — Produce the 3-minute WHY video that explains the brand's core purpose and mission — the cornerstone content asset of Social Amplification Engine Stage 3.
  • Create a 3x3 video grid — Build the 3×3 grid of nine videos — three core topics across three funnel levels — that gives a brand complete content coverage for SAE Stage 3.
  • Film casual team conversations — Record informal team discussions about problem-solving and client strategy so behind-the-scenes thinking becomes authentic raw content.
  • Film conference presentations — Record conference talks and audience Q&A so one speaking slot yields months of raw Content Factory material plus E-E-A-T proof of authority.
  • Map out one minute video topics using topic wheel — Use the Topic Wheel's WHY/HOW/WHAT rings to turn a brand's purpose and real customer questions into a systematic one-minute video recording queue.
  • Record one minute videos — Record short, unscripted one-minute videos on a phone answering real customer questions — the raw fuel every Content Factory pipeline run starts from.
  • Record screen shares of real audits — Capture screen recordings walking through actual client audits so the analysis work you already do becomes teachable, publishable proof of expertise.
  • Set up content library — Organize all raw and processed content into one structured, trackable library so the Content Factory has a single intake and nothing gets lost (SAE Stage 3).

Content Factory — Process

Transforming raw content into finished assets using AI tools and the Blog Posting Guidelines

  • Create dollar a day ad creatives — Format proven content into ad creatives for Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube so the Promote stage can put $1/day behind validated winners.
  • Create quote cards from strongest statements — Turn the video's best verbatim soundbites into attributed visual quote cards for social posting and article imagery.
  • Create social media posts per platform — Write platform-native social posts (Facebook, LinkedIn, Instagram, Twitter/X) from one piece of processed content — reframed per platform, never copy-pasted across.
  • Extract 15 60 second clips from long form video — Cut a long-form recording into 15–60 second standalone clips that feed social posts, quote cards, and Dollar a Day creatives.
  • Grade article using jennifer — Run a finished article through the Jennifer grading system for a quality score and fix what it flags before the piece moves to the Post stage.
  • Process videos via marketscale — Use the MarketScale platform to process and distribute raw video as an alternate Process-stage path, returning finished assets to the Content Library.
  • Step 1 upload video to google drive and descript — Move a raw video into the designated Google Drive folder and a Descript project so the Content Factory Process stage can begin (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 1).
  • Step 10 embed source video — Embed the original source video in the article so readers get the first-hand footage alongside the written version (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 10).
  • Step 11 proofread with grammarly or chatgpt — Run the finished draft through Grammarly or ChatGPT for grammar, tone, and readability — without letting the tools sand off the speaker's voice (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 11).
  • Step 2 transcribe video using descript — Produce a clean, accurate verbatim transcript in Descript — correcting every blue-underlined low-confidence word — so the article is written from what was actually said (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 2).
  • Step 3 watch video and identify gct — Watch the full video and pin down Goals, Content, and Targeting — in that order — so every downstream writing decision serves a defined purpose (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 3).
  • Step 4 research edit add timestamps and outline — Research the topic, clean the transcript, mark timestamps at topic shifts, and build the H2/H3 outline that the article will be written into (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 4).
  • Step 5 write article from transcript — Transform the cleaned transcript into a structured article that keeps the speaker's voice and stories while following GCT (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 5).
  • Step 6 write title and headings — Write an SEO title under 60 characters, a meta description under 160, and descriptive H2/H3 subheadings built on the focus keyword (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 6).
  • Step 7 write hook and establish context — Write an opening hook that earns the reader's next paragraph — establishing context, relevance, and the focus keyword in the first lines (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 7).
  • Step 8 add photos and featured image — Add real photos — never stock — and set a unique featured image so the article carries visual proof instead of decoration (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 8).
  • Step 9 add internal links with proper anchor text — Insert internal links using the entity-linking decision tree — people to personal sites, companies to company sites, concepts to definitive articles — with descriptive 3–6 word anchors (Blog Posting Guidelines Step 9).
  • Use descript underlord to remove filler words — Use Descript's Underlord to strip filler words (uh, um, like, you know) from the video and transcript while preserving natural human cadence.
  • Write email newsletter from video key insight — Draft an email newsletter built on the video's single key insight, driving the list back to the full article on the brand's site.
  • Write meta article documenting agent work — Use the Meta-Article Prompt Template to document a real AI-agent task run as a publishable article — the proof layer that turns work into examples.

Content Factory — Post

Publishing and distributing finished assets across platforms with proper linking

  • Email and dm client about published article — Notify the client by email and direct message that their article is live (Step 15), with the links and a specific ask that turns them into the first amplifier.
  • Place content on seo tree with proper links — Position the new piece at its correct spot in the SEO Tree hierarchy and wire the upward internal links (leaf → branch → trunk → root) before it goes live.
  • Post to facebook page — Share the new article or video natively on the Facebook business page in the owner's voice, creating the page post that Dollar a Day boosts will later run behind.
  • Post to linkedin — Publish the new piece to LinkedIn with professional first-person framing from the owner's profile, turning the article into a lesson the owner's network will share.
  • Set wordpress author to correct person — Make the site owner the byline on every post — not an admin account or VA — so Google attributes the content to the right entity and E-E-A-T accrues to the brand.
  • Share in 44k facebook group — Post the new piece into the 44,000-member Facebook group as a lesson for the community (Step 16), generating the first wave of organic engagement signal.
  • Step 12 post article on wordpress — Publish a finished Content Factory article on the client's WordPress site in the Gutenberg block editor with clean formatting, ready for categorization, SEO, and distribution.
  • Step 13 categorize post and add tags — Assign the WordPress post to its SEO Tree branch category and add attribute tags so readers, agents, and the REST API can pull it by topic, stage, and entity.
  • Step 14a configure rankmath seo plugin — Set the focus keyword, SEO title, and meta description in RankMath and tune the post until it scores 70+ so the article competes in search from day one.
  • Step 14b run linkwhisper for internal links — Use LinkWhisper to surface and implement internal link suggestions in both directions, so the new post is woven into the SEO Tree instead of published as an orphan.
  • Step 17 final formatting and qa checks — Run the final formatting, link, and quality pass across the live article and its distribution so the piece is verifiably done before handing off to the Promote stage.
  • Upload processed video to youtube — Publish the edited Content Factory video to the brand's YouTube channel with optimized title, description, and tags, linked back to the article it became.
  • Verify all items on blog posting checklist — Walk the complete Blog Posting verification checklist item by item as a binary gate, so nothing publishes (or ships to Promote) with a known miss.

Content Factory — Promote

Amplification through ads, social engagement, and Dollar a Day campaigns

  • Apply metrics decomposition — Break a topline campaign metric into its component drivers (SAE Stage 6) so you can name exactly which lever — creative, targeting, or landing page — moved the result.
  • Boost top 3 5 facebook posts — Identify the 3–5 best-performing organic Facebook posts and put paid budget behind them (SAE Stage 5), so money only ever amplifies proven content.
  • Compare current vs last period performance — Run the period-over-period comparison (SAE Stage 6) that separates real trends from noise, so kill/scale decisions rest on direction, not single data points.
  • Create and send thank you machine videos — Record and send one-minute personalized thank-you videos as part of amplification — public gratitude that the recipient shares, compounding reach for free.
  • Create new saved audiences from audience insights — Mine Facebook audience insights for who actually engages, then build named saved audiences that feed switch boosts and new $1/day tests (SAE Stage 6).
  • Engage with social comments — Reply to every real comment on boosted and organic posts in the owner's voice — engagement lowers ad costs, surfaces new video topics, and produces testimonial material.
  • Execute switch boost to target new audiences — Extend a proven winner to new audience segments by switching the boost's targeting while keeping the same post — social proof compounds on one post object.
  • List top 3 5 recommendations for next 7 days — Distill the week's analysis into 3–5 force-ranked, owner-assigned actions for the next 7 days — the deliverable that closes the SAE Stage 6 optimization loop.
  • Post roundup listicle content — Aggregate the period's proven winners into a roundup or listicle that concentrates internal links on top content and gives audiences one shareable entry point.
  • Review budget allocation by channel — Audit how spend is distributed across channels, ad sets, and funnel levels against performance, then reallocate by Dollar a Day rules — starve losers, feed winners ≤2× per step.
  • Run dollar a day campaign on winning content — Put $1/day per ad set behind validated, high-performing content across layered audiences — maximum learning per dollar, then kill losers and scale winners.
  • Sequence content cold warm conversion — Arrange winning content into a cold → warm → conversion funnel so strangers meet the WHY first, engagers get depth, and only warm audiences see the offer.
  • Set up remarketing ads for landing page abandoners — Build Google and Facebook remarketing ads aimed at 1-day landing page abandoners (SAE Stage 5) — the hottest audience you have, recovered for $1/day.

Digital Plumbing

Technical infrastructure that must be in place before any marketing can succeed

  • Add click to call links for mobile — Make every displayed phone number a tap-to-dial tel: link on mobile, tracked as a conversion event.
  • Add schema markup person localbusiness — Implement Person or LocalBusiness JSON-LD with sameAs links to every verified profile, stitching the brand into one machine-readable entity.
  • Claim and brand facebook page — Put the Facebook business page under the owner's control with correct admin structure, real branding, and NAP that matches the canon.
  • Configure https with no mixed content — Serve the entire site over HTTPS with zero mixed-content warnings so browsers, Google, and visitors trust every page.
  • Configure spf dkim dmarc for deliverability — Publish SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records so the domain's email authenticates, lands in inboxes instead of spam, and can't be trivially spoofed.
  • Convert instagram to professional account — Switch Instagram to a professional account connected to the Facebook page, with handle, bio, and photo matching the brand canon.
  • Create clear conversion path — Build one obvious, trackable path from landing to conversion (call, form, or booking) so visitors never wonder what to do next.
  • Create linkedin page matching business entity — Set up a LinkedIn presence whose name and details exactly match the business entity, strengthening the entity graph Google reads.
  • Create xml sitemap and reference in robots txt — Generate a clean XML sitemap, reference it in robots.txt, and submit it to Search Console so Google can find every page worth indexing.
  • Ensure nap consistency across platforms — Make the business Name, Address, and Phone identical character-for-character across every platform so Google can trust the entity.
  • Ensure proper dns records — Audit the domain's DNS zone so A/CNAME records point at the live site, MX at the mail provider, and TXT records carry verifications — with no stale leftovers.
  • Ensure robots meta not blocking indexing — Verify no robots meta tag, header, or WordPress setting is silently telling Google not to index the site.
  • Ensure site loads under 3 seconds on mobile — Get the website loading in under 3 seconds on a mobile device, because slow plumbing wastes every ad dollar and ranking the site earns.
  • Ensure working contact form delivers notifications — Prove the contact form actually submits and that notifications land in an inbox the owner reads — because a silent form is a lead funeral.
  • Install google tag manager container — Install one Google Tag Manager container on every page of the site so all future tracking (GA4, Meta pixel, call events) deploys without editing code.
  • Install meta pixel with standard events — Install the Meta pixel on every page with standard events (Lead, Contact) on the actions that matter, so Dollar a Day campaigns can optimize and retarget.
  • Set correct business categories — Select the most accurate primary and secondary Google Business Profile categories so the listing ranks for the searches that actually drive revenue.
  • Set favicon — Upload a proper branded favicon so the site stops showing a default icon in browser tabs, bookmarks, and search results.
  • Set up call tracking for phone conversions — Implement call tracking with dynamic number insertion so phone calls — often the real conversion for local businesses — get measured without breaking NAP.
  • Set up consistent headshots and bios across profiles — Apply one professional headshot and one canonical bio across every profile so humans and Google recognize a single consistent entity.
  • Set up ga4 with internal traffic filtering — Stand up a correctly-owned GA4 property that collects on every page and filters out the owner's and team's own visits, so the numbers mean something.
  • Set up professional email on domain — Move the business off @gmail.com onto name@itsowndomain.com with a real mail provider, so every email reinforces the brand and deliverability can be authenticated.
  • Set up youtube channel with proper branding — Create and brand a YouTube channel connected to the website, ready to host the one-minute videos the Content Factory produces.
  • Upload real photos and set business hours — Load the Google Business Profile with recent real photos (no stock) and make the posted hours match reality, including holidays.
  • Verify domain ownership and registrar access — Confirm the business actually owns its domain and can log into the registrar — the single point of failure under everything else in digital plumbing.
  • Verify google business profile — Complete Google's verification of the Google Business Profile and put admin control in the business owner's own hands, so the profile can rank in Maps and anchor the entity.
  • Verify google search console and connect to ga4 — Verify Search Console ownership of the domain and link it to GA4 so search queries, impressions, and clicks flow into one measurement view.
  • Write proper title tags and meta descriptions — Give every indexable page a unique title tag under 60 characters and meta description under 160 characters, each carrying its focus keyword.

Dollar a Day Campaigns

The Dollar a Day advertising strategy: test at $1/day, find winners, scale gradually

  • Analyze cost per result and engagement — Review cost per result, relevance, engagement rate, and conversions per ad set against written targets — the Metrics and Analysis of MAA that turn $7 tests into decisions.
  • Boost one minute videos for personal branding — Run $1/day behind proven one-minute answer videos from the Public Figure page so a person's face and expertise keep showing up for exactly the people who should know them.
  • Build audience layers — Layer location, age, demographics, interests, and custom audiences into discrete, one-variable-apart ad-set targets so every $1/day test result is readable.
  • Create facebook instagram campaigns with location targeting — Build Facebook/Instagram campaigns layered by location, demographics, and interests at $1/day per ad set — the local-service workhorse of the Dollar a Day method.
  • Create youtube campaigns using aducate model — Set up YouTube ad campaigns with creative structured on the ADUCATE framework — educate before you ask — targeting layered audiences at minimum viable spend.
  • Execute switch boosts to new audiences — Point an already-winning creative at new untested audience segments via switch boosts — keeping the original post and its social proof — for new reach without new production.
  • Identify signals worth amplifying — Read engagement, watch time, shares, and comments across recent organic posts to find the proven winners that deserve $1/day amplification — boost winners, never hopes.
  • Kill underperforming ads — Cut every ad set that misses its target cost per result — on schedule, without sentiment — so budget concentrates on winners and losers cost $7 instead of $700.
  • Run multiple simultaneous tests — Launch many $1/day ad sets at once — different creatives, audiences, and placements, one variable apart — so winners reveal themselves in a week instead of a quarter.
  • Run twitter x promotion for thought leader threads — Boost a thought leader's proven threads and key tweets on Twitter/X so winning ideas reach decision-makers beyond the follower graph — same Dollar a Day discipline, X edition.
  • Scale winners by increasing budget gradually — Grow budget on proven winners gradually — never more than 2× per adjustment — so results scale without resetting delivery learning or breaking the economics that made them winners.
  • Sequence content from awareness to conversion — Chain audiences cold → engagement → warm → conversion so $1/day viewers are remarketed down the funnel instead of being pitched on first touch.
  • Set 1 day budget per ad set — Set the minimum viable spend — $1/day per ad set — so each test buys real performance data with zero meaningful risk, and a 20-cell matrix costs less than one bad boost.
  • Set up digital plumbing pixels tracking — Stand up the tracking layer — verified profiles, working pixels, end-to-end conversion tracking — before the first dollar is spent, so every $1/day test produces readable data.
  • Set up meta business manager and public figure pages — Configure Meta Business Manager under the owner's control and stand up a Public Figure page so a personal brand can run Dollar a Day cleanly, with the person — not just the company — accruing the audience.
  • Set up tiktok campaigns — Configure TikTok ads through the six-part Dollar a Day sequence — plumbing, goals, content, targeting, amplification, optimization — using native vertical video that already won.
  • Use dollar a day to influence media coverage — Aim $1/day ad sets at the journalists, editors, podcasters, and producers who cover your space, so your proof and stories earn familiarity first and press mentions second.
  • Verify prerequisites product customers content — Gate every Dollar a Day launch on the three prerequisites — a valued product, happy customers, and strong content — so ad spend amplifies a working business instead of subsidizing a broken one.

Website QA Audit

Systematic three-layer review: Digital Plumbing, Content Architecture, Authority & Trust

  • Audit internal links between all blog posts — Checks that every blog post links to at least one other post and a service page, so no content is orphaned and link equity flows toward money pages.
  • Check all cta buttons lead to correct destinations — Click-tests every call-to-action button on the site to confirm each routes to the destination its copy promises, keeping the conversion path unbroken.
  • Check each homepage section includes relevant image — Walks the homepage section by section to confirm each one carries a contextually relevant image, so no part of the page falls back to a wall of text.
  • Check favicon is set — Confirms a proper branded favicon is uploaded and rendering in browser tabs, bookmarks, and search results instead of a blank or default icon.
  • Check for broken links — Scans the entire site with a link checker to find and fix every broken internal and external link before visitors or crawlers hit them.
  • Check lead magnet section has visual mockup — Confirms the lead magnet offer is shown with a visual mockup or preview image, because an offer people can see converts better than a text link.
  • Check meta description under 160 chars — Audits every page's meta description for the under-160-character limit and keyword relevance so search snippets display fully and earn the click.
  • Check schema connects to all verified profiles — Confirms the schema sameAs array covers every active, verified profile the owner has — the completeness pass that closes the entity loop for Google.
  • Check seo title under 60 chars with focus keyword — Audits every page's SEO title for the under-60-character limit and focus keyword inclusion so titles display fully in search and rank for the right terms.
  • Check social profiles linked and prominent — Verifies the owner's social profiles are linked from the site, working, and prominently placed, so visitors and Google can follow the entity off-site.
  • Ensure no stock images used — Audits all site imagery to confirm photos are real — the owner, their team, their work — because stock photography destroys the authenticity a personal brand runs on.
  • Ensure no text only sections spanning full viewport — Scans every page for sections that fill an entire screen with nothing but text, the wall-of-words pattern that makes visitors bounce.
  • Ensure robots meta not blocking indexing qa audit — Confirms no robots meta tags, headers, or WordPress settings are silently blocking search engines from indexing the site's money pages.
  • Test mobile load time under 3 seconds — Measures real mobile load speed with PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse to confirm the site loads in under 3 seconds, where most local-service visitors arrive.
  • Verify achievements are evidenced not just claimed — Audits every expertise and results claim on the site for attached proof — links, photos, screenshots, named sources — converting assertions into E-E-A-T evidence.
  • Verify all images have descriptive alt text — Audits every image on the site for meaningful, descriptive alt text so images are accessible, indexable, and reinforce each page's topic.
  • Verify all pages written in first person — Confirms every page of a personal-brand site speaks as the owner ("I" and "my"), not about them in third person, so the site reads as the person and not a brochure.
  • Verify at least one video embed on homepage — Confirms the homepage carries at least one working embedded video of the owner, adding motion, face, and voice to the first impression.
  • Verify email opt in form exists and works — Confirms an email capture form is present, submits cleanly, and actually delivers the subscriber and notification, so the site builds a list the owner controls.
  • Verify entity linking follows decision tree — Audits all internal and outbound links against the entity linking decision tree — people to personal sites, companies to company sites, concepts to definitive articles.
  • Verify featured images unique per blog post — Confirms every blog post carries its own unique featured image so archives, social shares, and search results don't show a wall of identical thumbnails.
  • Verify footer includes social links and secondary nav — Confirms the site footer carries working social media links and a secondary navigation so every page ends with somewhere useful to go.
  • Verify ga4 configured with internal traffic filtered — Confirms the GA4 property is correctly installed and excludes the owner's and team's own visits, so audit and campaign data reflect real visitors only.
  • Verify google business profile is verified — Confirms the Google Business Profile tied to the site is actually verified by Google and under the owner's control, anchoring local trust signals.
  • Verify gtm installed and firing on every page — Confirms Google Tag Manager is present and firing site-wide so every downstream tracking tag works, for any personal-brand or local-service site under audit.
  • Verify hero section contains real photo of person — Confirms the homepage hero leads with a real photograph of the brand owner — not stock, not an illustration — so visitors meet the person immediately.
  • Verify https with no mixed content — Confirms the entire site serves over HTTPS with zero mixed-content warnings, protecting visitor trust and preventing browser security flags.
  • Verify meta pixel installed with lead events — Confirms the Meta pixel loads on every page and fires standard Lead events on key actions, so Dollar a Day campaigns can optimize and retarget correctly.
  • Verify minimum 5 distinct images on homepage — Counts the unique, relevant images on the homepage and confirms there are at least five, the minimum visual density for a credible personal-brand site.
  • Verify person schema with sameas links — Validates that Person schema markup exists, parses cleanly, and carries sameAs links to the owner's social profiles, tying the personal brand together as one entity for Google.
  • Verify rank math score above 70 on every page — Runs the RankMath on-page audit across all posts and pages to confirm every one scores 70/100 or higher, the site's minimum on-page SEO bar.
  • Verify social proof photos present — Confirms social proof sections show real photographic evidence — client photos, job results, event shots — not text-only claims that anyone could type.
  • Verify testimonials include headshots with attribution — Checks that every testimonial on the site pairs a real headshot with a name and attribution, so praise is visibly from real people.
  • Verify testimonials with full attribution — Holds every testimonial to the full trust standard — name, title, company, and headshot — so any skeptical visitor could verify the person is real.
  • Verify wordpress author set to site owner name — Confirms every post is attributed to the site owner's author account — not admin, VA, or agency logins — so authorship signals accrue to the person's entity.
  • Verify xml sitemap exists and in robotstxt — Confirms a current XML sitemap exists, is referenced in robots.txt, and is submitted to Google Search Console so every page can be found and indexed.

SEO & Content Architecture

Building the SEO Tree, internal linking, and content hierarchy for organic authority

  • Build external backlinks from authoritative sources — Earn backlinks from publications, podcasts, and industry sites Google already trusts — never bought, always descriptive-anchored, and routed to the right page by the entity-linking decision tree.
  • Enhance existing articles before creating new ones — Force a search-first discipline on all content proposals — improve the page that already owns a concept instead of publishing thin new competitors, keeping effort on Greatest Hits rather than net-new bets.
  • Fit every new piece into the tree structure — Classify every new piece of content as a leaf, a branch, or an enhancement to an existing hub before it publishes, so nothing ships outside the SEO Tree and no page ever competes with its own hub.
  • Follow entity linking decision tree — Route every entity mentioned in a piece of content to its correct destination — people to personal sites, companies to company sites, concepts to definitive articles — so links build the entity graph instead of noise.
  • Identify and eliminate orphan standalone articles — Find every page with zero internal links pointing to it and give each one a disposition — connect it to its branch, consolidate it into the owning hub, or cut it — until the site has no orphans.
  • Link leaves to branches branches to trunk — Wire the SEO Tree together with upward internal links — every leaf links to its branch, every branch to the trunk and root — so authority flows from examples up to hubs and money pages.
  • Map content hierarchically root trunk branches leaves — Organize every page on a site into the SEO Tree — root (homepage/entity), trunk (brand), branches (definitive articles), leaves (case studies and meta-articles) — so one coherent hierarchy replaces a pile of disconnected posts.
  • Remove duplicate thin pages — Identify pages that are thin or duplicate another page's topic, then merge them into the owning hub or remove them with proper redirects, so no two pages ever compete for one concept.
  • Replace ai looking content with real eeat — Rewrite generic AI-generated pages with real experience, named examples, and third-party proof so every page demonstrates E-E-A-T instead of merely claiming expertise.
  • Step 1 inventory content and establish gct per page — Audit every URL on the site into one inventory and write Goals, Content, Targeting for each major page — the foundation document the entire 6-step internal linking process runs on.
  • Step 2 fix categories and tags in wordpress — Rebuild WordPress taxonomy so categories mirror the SEO Tree branches and tags carry cross-cutting attributes, making the site's structure legible to readers, Google, and agents querying the REST API.
  • Step 3 identify money pages and orphan pages — Mark the revenue-driving pages that need internal-link support and the orphan pages that need connecting, producing the prioritized source-to-target link plan that Step 4 executes.
  • Step 4 create links with proper anchor text and placement — Execute the link plan — contextual internal links placed in body content with 3–6 word descriptive anchors, routed by the entity-linking decision tree, flowing upward through the SEO Tree.
  • Step 5 qa links against google guidelines — Audit every link created in Step 4 against Google's quality guidelines — anchors, placement, relevance, volume, and breakage — so nothing manipulative or broken ships to measurement.
  • Step 6 measure and iterate with maa — Close the internal linking loop with the MAA cycle — baseline, measure rankings and traffic per page, analyze which links worked, act on the findings, and re-run the 6-step process on schedule.
  • Use proper anchor text 3 6 words descriptive — Enforce the house anchor-text rule on every link — 3–6 descriptive words that predict the destination — and purge generic anchors like 'click here' from the entire site.

Personal Branding

Making expertise findable, reputation defensible, referrals scalable

  • Add consistent headshots and bios across profiles — Put the same professional headshot and the same bio facts on every profile so Google reconciles one person, not five.
  • Build personal brand website — Build the entity-home website on yourname.com that answers who this person is, what they do, and why to trust them.
  • Build third party validation — Accumulate the independent proof — press, Wikipedia/Wikidata, podcasts, books — Google needs before it will trust the entity with a Knowledge Panel.
  • Claim and verify knowledge panel when it appears — Claim and verify the Google Knowledge Panel as soon as Google generates one, putting the person in control of their own entity card.
  • Create collaborative content with industry peers — Co-create content with respected peers so both parties earn authority, audience crossover, and reciprocal entity links.
  • Create dollar a day ad creatives from best content — Turn the proven top-performing personal-brand content into ad creatives ready for Dollar a Day amplification.
  • Create social content from video clips — Cut 15–60 second clips from the videos and publish platform-native social posts that point back to the brand site.
  • Establish entity identity — Lock one consistent name, domain, and headshot across the entire web so Google can reconcile a single entity worthy of a Knowledge Panel.
  • Generate backlinks and entity mentions — Earn links and name mentions from sources Google already trusts, consolidating authority on the personal-brand entity.
  • Get featured on podcasts — Secure podcast guest appearances that produce entity mentions, backlinks, and reusable content from sources Google already trusts.
  • Get mentioned in publications — Earn press mentions in recognized publications and media outlets — third-party proof for the entity and fuel for the Knowledge Panel.
  • Implement person schema with sameas links — Add Person schema JSON-LD to the entity home with a sameAs array binding every verified profile into one Google-readable entity.
  • Implement technical schema markup — Upgrade the entity's structured data — Person or Organization schema with a complete, verified sameAs graph — to Knowledge Panel grade.
  • Link all content back to personal brand site — Make sure every published piece — posts, articles, profiles, guest features — links back to yourname.com through proper entity linking.
  • Measure search impressions traffic inbound opportunities — Run the MAA loop on the personal brand — tracking search impressions, organic traffic, and inbound opportunities to prove the system compounds.
  • Record one minute videos answering customer questions — Produce short, unscripted phone videos answering real customer questions — the raw material for the whole personal-brand content engine.
  • Register personal name domain — Secure yourname.com as the permanent entity anchor every other personal-brand asset points back to.
  • Repurpose videos into blog posts via content factory — Run the one-minute videos through the Content Factory pipeline to publish structured blog posts on the personal brand site.
  • Run thank you machine for every interaction — Turn every meaningful interaction into public gratitude content that builds relationships and personal-brand authority simultaneously.
  • Secure guest appearances and speaking engagements — Land speaking slots at conferences, webinars, and events that build verifiable third-party authority for the personal brand.
  • Set up professional social profiles — Create or claim professional profiles on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and Twitter/X that all point back to the entity home.
  • Verify google business profile personal brand — Complete Google's verification of the Business Profile attached to the personal brand, where a qualifying business exists.

Strategy & Measurement

Nine Triangles Framework, MAA loop, Topic Wheel, Social Amplification Engine

  • Analyze why underperformers fail — Diagnose every underperforming campaign or content piece into one of three root causes — creative, targeting, or offer — so the fix targets the actual failure instead of guessing.
  • Content strategy 90 greatest hits 10 new — Allocate 90% of content and promotion effort to proven greatest hits and 10% to new experiments, so budget follows evidence instead of novelty.
  • Funnel levels audience engagement conversion — Map every marketing activity into the three funnel levels — Audience, Engagement, Conversion — so each campaign, post, and metric is judged at the stage it actually serves.
  • Gct goals content targeting — Define Goals, Content, and Targeting for every campaign and page before launch, so what you're trying to achieve, what you'll say, and who will see it are aligned and written down.
  • Kill underperformers scale winners — Act on the weekly diagnosis — cut campaigns and content that miss target, and gradually increase investment in what works — so budget continuously migrates toward proof.
  • Learn do teach apprentice model — Run the Learn, Do, Teach apprentice model — study the canonical material, execute the task under review, then teach it — so skills compound into team capacity instead of staying with one expert.
  • Maa cycle metrics analysis action — Run the continuous MAA cycle — Metrics, Analysis, Action — so every number collected leads to a diagnosis and every diagnosis leads to a concrete change.
  • Management communicate iterate delegate — Run the Management triangle — Communicate, Iterate, Delegate — to catch issues early, improve work through fast feedback, and hand off responsibility progressively.
  • Map how ring methods — Define the middle ring of the Topic Wheel — the named methods, frameworks, and approaches that deliver the WHY — creating the spokes every content topic will hang from.
  • Map what ring specific content — Define the outer ring of the Topic Wheel — the specific topics, customer questions, examples, and stories — so the brand has a concrete, mission-anchored content backlog.
  • Map why ring core purpose — Define the innermost ring of the Topic Wheel — the core mission and reason the brand exists — so every method and content piece in the outer rings traces back to one purpose.
  • Measure content rankings traffic revenue — Track the full pipeline from content published to rankings to traffic to revenue in one table, so you can see exactly which stage is broken instead of arguing about "marketing."
  • Personal efficiency do delegate delete — Apply the Personal Efficiency triangle — Do, Delegate, Delete — so every incoming item is actioned immediately, handed off with instructions, or removed, keeping the operator at inbox zero.
  • Show whats working and what isnt — Present performance data as an explicit two-column verdict — winners with evidence, underperformers with evidence — so the weekly report drives kill/scale decisions instead of hiding them.
  • Stage 1 plumbing fb ads google ads analytics — Complete Stage 1 of the Social Amplification Engine — Facebook Ads account, Google Ads linked via MCC, and Google Analytics — so every later stage runs on verified tracking instead of guesses.
  • Stage 2 goals mission 90 day goals cpa roas budget — Complete Stage 2 of the Social Amplification Engine — define the mission (start with WHY), 90-day goals, target CPA/ROAS, and budget — so amplification has numbers to optimize toward.
  • Stage 3 content endorsements why video 3x3 grid library — Complete Stage 3 of the Social Amplification Engine — assemble endorsements, produce the WHY video and 3×3 video grid, and organize everything into a Content Library ready to amplify.
  • Stage 4 targeting custom audiences lookalikes remarketing — Complete Stage 4 of the Social Amplification Engine — import custom audiences, build interest targets, create lookalikes, and set remarketing pools — so Stage 5 boosts reach people who can actually buy.
  • Stage 5 amplification boost posts remarketing ads — Complete Stage 5 of the Social Amplification Engine — boost the top 3–5 proven posts, run the 4-stage boost optimization, and switch on remarketing ads — putting paid spend only behind organic winners.
  • Stage 6 optimization metrics budget review recommendations — Complete Stage 6 of the Social Amplification Engine — decompose metrics, compare periods, audit budget allocation, and deliver the top 3–5 actions for the next 7 days — closing the loop back into the engine.
  • Submit weekly maa report every friday — Deliver the weekly MAA report every Friday — same metrics, what's working vs not, why, and the actions taken — so clients and the team see decisions, not just numbers.
  • Systematization content checklists software — Move any recurring task up the Systematization ladder — from documented content, to a checklist anyone can run, to software/agent automation — so quality stops depending on memory.
  • Three pillars marketing operations finance — Map the business onto its Three Pillars — Marketing, Operations, Finance — assign one health metric to each, and rebalance effort toward the weakest pillar.
  • Use for content planning and video topics — Turn the completed Topic Wheel into a prioritized one-minute video recording plan, so content production runs from a mission-anchored backlog instead of day-of improvisation.
  • Google ads analyzer — Analyze Google Ads (Search, PMax, Video, Display) data using the MAA framework for local service businesses.

Thank You Machine

Turn gratitude into content, testimonials, and referrals — a compounding asset

  • Boost with dollar a day — Put $1/day behind the public thank-you post so it reaches people similar to the one thanked — gratitude doubles as targeting, and proof outperforms pitch.
  • Build into post project checklist and weekly routine — Systematize gratitude — wire thank-you steps into the post-project checklist and a standing weekly routine so the machine runs on process, not memory or mood.
  • Identify thank you triggers — Flag every moment worth public gratitude — milestones, referrals, reviews, advice, problem-solving — into a running log so no thank-you-worthy event slips by unrecorded.
  • Process through content factory — Run the thank-you video through the Content Factory to mint five assets — social post, blog excerpt, testimonial, ad creative, and email — from one minute of gratitude.
  • Record one minute thank you video — Shoot a one-minute, unscripted phone video that names the person, says exactly what they did, and explains why it mattered — sincerity is the production value.
  • Tag the person on social media — Post the thank-you publicly and tag the person so they see it, feel it, and share it — their reshare carries your gratitude to their entire audience with their endorsement attached.

Knowledge System Maintenance

The recursive maintenance layer — auditing and improving SOPs and definitive articles

  • Add knowledge capture to existing workflows — Embed an explicit Knowledge Capture step inside the Zoom call, campaign review, and audit workflows so insights are captured by process, not by memory.
  • Add last audited column to definitive article guide status table — Add a Last Audited column to the Definitive Article Guide status table, initialized to "Not yet audited" for every article, so audit recency becomes visible and enforceable.
  • Apply portability discipline to meta articles — Ensure meta-articles separate the transferable general approach from the platform-specific details of that particular run, so documented agent work stays useful after tools change.
  • Audit this maintenance article every 6 months — Run the 6-month recursive audit of /knowledge-system-maintenance itself — test its five processes against six months of reality and amend, add, or retire them through their own protocol.
  • Check information currency with latest data — Refresh a definitive article's facts, numbers, and tool steps against the latest GBP, Ahrefs, client Zoom, and campaign data so the page never teaches stale reality.
  • Check topic coherence for each definitive article — Run the SEO Tree test on a definitive article so every element provably supports its declared topic — the first check of the quarterly audit.
  • Conduct first portability review of blog posting guidelines — Run the pilot portability review on the Blog Posting Guidelines — separate its methodology from its tool-specific steps and document the worked pattern for every other SOP.
  • Create knowledge capture note template and distribute — Build the Knowledge Capture Note template and distribute it into the team's actual workflows, then confirm real notes start flowing — the implementation milestone for Process 3.
  • Create shared location for sop amendment proposals — Stand up the single shared location — Google Doc, WordPress draft, or project board — where all SOP Amendment Proposals live, get queued, and get resolved.
  • Create sop amendment proposal 500 words — Turn a real-world SOP failure into a ≤500-word amendment proposal — problem, affected SOP, exact proposed language, and at least two real examples — ready for weekly senior review.
  • Generate knowledge capture note within 24 hours — Capture any reusable insight as a four-field Knowledge Capture Note (source, insight, destination, priority) within 24 hours, before context decays and the lesson is lost.
  • Provide knowledge capture note template — Author the canonical Knowledge Capture Note template — source, insight in 1–3 sentences, destination, priority — so capturing an insight takes two minutes, not a decision.
  • Review existing sops for portability compliance — Audit the existing SOP library for methodology/implementation separation, grade each document, and queue the entangled ones for restructuring.
  • Review past 6 months of sop amendment proposals for patterns — Mine six months of SOP Amendment Proposals for patterns — repeatedly-patched SOPs, missing processes, proposal friction — and convert each pattern into a system-level fix.
  • Route notes to sop update protocol or directly to articles — Move every Knowledge Capture Note to its correct destination — into the SOP Update Protocol when it changes how work is done, or directly into a definitive article when it adds facts.
  • Schedule and assign quarterly auditors — Assign a named auditor and deadline to every definitive article each quarter so the Process 1 audit actually happens instead of remaining a good intention.
  • Schedule first 6 month recursive review — Put the first 6-month recursive review of the knowledge maintenance system on the calendar with named reviewers and a prep package, arming the self-improvement loop.
  • Schedule first quarterly audit cycle — Launch the first quarterly definitive-article audit cycle — every article assigned to a named auditor with Q3 2026 as the deadline — turning Process 1 from spec into schedule.
  • Senior team member reviews weekly — Run the weekly senior review of queued SOP Amendment Proposals — approve with version increment and changelog, or reject with a written reason — so SOPs evolve under control.
  • Separate methodology layer what why from implementation layer how — Split a process document into a tool-agnostic methodology layer (what/why) and a platform-specific implementation layer (how) so platform churn never invalidates the method.
  • Set up sop amendment proposal tracking system — Stand up the end-to-end SOP Amendment tracking system — shared location, tracking fields, weekly review slot, and a verified dry run — so Process 2 operates from day one.
  • Tag proposal with affected sop and queue for review — Tag each SOP Amendment Proposal with its affected SOP and place it in the weekly review queue so no proposal is lost, mislabeled, or invisible to the reviewer.
  • Update status table in definitive article guide — Close out an article's quarterly audit by stamping its Green/Yellow/Red status and audit date into the Definitive Article Guide status table so the dashboard stays true.
  • Validate cross reference integrity across articles — Verify that every other article linking to the audited definitive article still describes it accurately — and that its own outbound cross-links do the same.
  • Verify structural completeness against 8 step framework — Confirm a definitive article contains every required structural element — framework steps, TOC-to-content match, live links, accurate schema — during the quarterly audit.

Gaps & Tasks to Create

Articles and SOPs that need to be written — prioritized by impact

  • How to configure ga4 with internal traffic filtering — Create the missing definitive article — the complete GA4 setup guide covering property creation and internal traffic filtering, so analytics reflect customers instead of the team (High-priority gap).
  • How to convert third person copy to first person — Create the missing definitive article — the process for rewriting personal brand website copy from third-person corporate voice into authentic first person (Medium-priority gap).
  • How to grade an article using jennifer — Create the missing definitive article — the guide to scoring article quality with the Jennifer grading system, the quality gate in the Content Factory's Process stage (Low-priority gap).
  • How to install and configure gtm — Create the missing definitive article — a step-by-step guide to installing and configuring a Google Tag Manager container on any website (High-priority gap; also closes the open Digital Plumbing GTM task).
  • How to map your seo tree from scratch — Create the missing definitive article — a step-by-step guide to building an SEO Tree for a new or existing website, extending the /seo-tree concept hub with the from-scratch how-to (Medium-priority gap).
  • How to process videos via marketscale — Create the missing definitive article — the SOP for processing videos through MarketScale and distributing them across platforms, documented as an alternative lane in the Content Factory's Process stage (Low-priority gap).
  • How to run the ai business audit — Create the missing definitive article — the SOP for using AI tools to audit a business's digital presence systematically across plumbing, content architecture, and authority (Medium-priority gap).
  • How to set up schema markup for personal brand site — Create the missing definitive article — a guide to implementing Person schema with sameAs links that bind a personal brand's site and social profiles into one entity Google can recognize (High-priority gap).
